title: Monument and marker register — spatial verification layer
status: PROPOSAL — audit-spatial-1 branch; reliability profile is an ED candidate (see docs/reconstruction/audit/spatial-evidence.md), not self-adopted
frame: battlefield-local meters: local_x = UTM 18N (EPSG:26918) easting - 304208.0216360274, local_z = northing - 4404534.271124143 (data/heightmap/heightmap.json origin; identical to the landmark-anchors convention)

coverage.included: July 3 afternoon assault sector: full II Corps front (Webb, Hall, Harrow, Smyth, Willard/Sherrill brigades + Hazard's batteries + Cowan), Stannard's brigade, 8th Ohio, CSA state memorials on the Pickett's Charge start line, 11th MS / 26th NC advance markers, Armistead marker; SOUTHERN FIELD (pass 6, 2026-07-11): Wheatfield/Rose Woods/Devil's Den cluster (Caldwell's four brigade tablets + 5th NH/Irish Bde/4th MI/17th ME/124th NY monuments + Zook statue), Smith's 4th NY two-position pair, the four CSA ladder brigade tablets (W/S Confederate Ave) + Semmes-mw/Anderson-w/Benning/Robertson/Law advance markers, the Little Round Top summit cluster (Vincent-mw, Weed+Hazlett, 140th NY, 20th ME, 44th NY, 16th MI, 83rd PA, 91st PA, 146th NY, 155th PA + both brigade tablets), 1st Minnesota pair + Willard-KIA marker; PEACH ORCHARD / PLUM RUN PERIPHERY (pass 7, 2026-07-11): III Corps salient tablets (Graham + Carr/Brewster/Burling + Humphreys division pair-flagged) + Sickles-wounded and Rogers-house pins + 73rd NY (4th Excelsior) + Humphreys statue, the V Corps regulars pair (Day/Burbank + Ayres division), PA Reserves (McCandless + Crawford division/statue), Wheaton/Nevin, and the Anderson-division pair (Wright tablet + Cemetery Ridge advance marker; Perry WCA + Emmitsburg-Rd advanced tablets)
coverage.notIncluded: right/left flank marker stones (see flankMarkerGap), McGilvery's line, Cemetery Hill/Culp's Hill fronts, July 1 field, cavalry fields — future passes
coverage.flankMarkerGap: GNMP flank-marker stones (R.F./L.F.) physically exist for most Union regiments here, but no license-clean machine-readable coordinate source was found this pass: Stone Sentinels does not publish their coordinates, HMDB is bot-walled (Cloudflare) and catalogs few of the small stones, OpenStreetMap has essentially none mapped in this sector (2 flank-named objects park-wide), and the NPS 'Monumentation' ArcGIS layer is survey benchmarks, not war monuments. Honest coverage: 0 flank-marker entries. Recommended fill: manual HMDB browser pass or field GPS; the secondary/advance/position markers captured here partially serve the same line-extent purpose.
coverage.southernFieldChannelNote: Pass-6 extraction channels: CSA and most Union brigade-tablet pages on Stone Sentinels still print DMS coordinates (dms-text); Stone Sentinels regiment/monument pages served NO machine-readable coordinates through the 2026-07-11 fetch channel, so those entries carry OpenStreetMap (ODbL) as the PRIMARY coordinate source (extraction osm-node/osm-way-center, per-entry note) - a recorded deviation from the register's dms-text default. OSM was the register's calibrated cross-check source (ED-39); typical SS-vs-OSM deltas in this batch run 1-27 m with two flagged >50 m (tab-us-ii-1-3, tab-us-ii-1-4).

sources.stone-sentinels: gettysburg.stonesentinels.com unit pages, accessed 2026-07-10; coordinates from published DMS text (dms-text), Google-Maps embed payloads (gmaps-embed-b64), or map query links (gmaps-q); DMS text granularity 0.1 arc-second (~3 m)
sources.openstreetmap: Overpass API extract of historic=memorial/monument in the battlefield bbox, accessed 2026-07-10, ODbL; used as an independent cross-check for dms-text entries and, in the pass-6 southern-field batch only, as the primary coordinate source where Stone Sentinels served no machine-readable coordinates (per-entry notes)

entries.positionSemantics: July 3 defense line at the wall west of the Copse (climax position)
July 3 position at the inner Angle wall (initial line, before fall-back and return)
entries.dataQuality: SUSPECT: Stone Sentinels publishes the identical DMS string for the 71st PA and 72nd PA main monuments (39°48'46.8"N 77°14'10.6"W) with near-identical location sentences — likely a copy-paste on the source page. The OSM cross-check (delta 41.5 m, flagged) supports the 71st monument standing NE of this point, nearer the Angle corner. Do not use this entry as an EC3 anchor without independent confirmation.

entries.positionSemantics: final countercharge position at the wall at The Angle (climax, ~15:25-15:30); site of the ED-4 litigation
entries.dataQuality: Shares its DMS string with the Stone Sentinels 71st PA page (see mon-71pa); the 72nd PA reading is the one consistent with OSM (delta 4.0 m), so this entry is retained as primary.

entries.positionSemantics: The charge's START line ('From the hill behind this monument on the morning of July Third 1863 the Second Massachusetts Infantry made an assault upon the Confederate troops in the works at the base of Culp's Hill opposite' — inscription verbatim, Stone Sentinels 2026-07-11). Dedicated 1879 — the field's FIRST permanent regimental monument. Strength/loss inscribed: 22 officers + 294 men; 4+41 k&mw, 6+84 w. NOTE: inscription says 'morning', no clock — the monument does NOT decide the CA-J3M-3 dispute.

source.name: OpenStreetMap (ODbL)
source.note: OSM/Overpass as PRIMARY coordinate source (the pass-6/7/8 channel deviation continues in pass 9: Stone Sentinels served inscriptions but no machine-readable coordinates). Pass-9 Spangler's Meadow / July 3 morning Culp's Hill cluster.

entries.positionSemantics: Marks 'the ground over which the left wing of the 27th Indiana advanced in a charge made by the regiment on the morning of July 3rd, 1863. Number engaged 339. Killed and wounded 110. Missing, one' (inscription verbatim) — 'morning', no clock, engaged=Fesler's 339 exactly.

source.name: OpenStreetMap (ODbL)

entries.positionSemantics: THE ONE CLOCK-BEARING STONE OF THE DISPUTE: 'This marks the farthest point gained by the Regiment in its charge at 6 a.m. July 3d. 1863 on the works at the base of the hill behind this tablet. Four color bearers were killed and four were wounded' (inscription verbatim) — the CA-J3M-3 EARLY pole's marker-grade attestation; ~80 m north of the main monument (the meadow crossing's depth).

entries.positionSemantics: Formation-line semantics: O'Neal's morning ground — j3-01 red label (6223, 5804) NE-of-hill arrival vs j3-02 (5721, 5694) north-slope attack state: the drawn ~510 m advance is the 8 a.m. wave-2 attack (or-27-2-oneal).
